display_name: AS-path prepend does nothing if weight or local-preference already decided best-path
context: AS-path prepending was applied to steer traffic away from a path, but the traffic still uses that path
observation: A path was prepended to make it less preferred, yet best-path selection still chooses it
naive_interpretation: Prepending is broken, or the prepend was not applied, so more prepends are needed
correct_interpretation: AS-path length is only one step in the best-path algorithm, and several higher-priority steps run first: weight, then local-preference, then locally-originated, then AS-path length. If weight or local-preference already favors the path, the algorithm decides before it ever compares AS-path length, so prepend has no effect no matter how many ASNs you add. Prepend also only influences inbound path selection on the neighbor that receives it. Identify which tie-breaker actually decided best-path before adding more prepends.
why: RFC 4271 best-path comparison is ordered; weight (vendor) and LOCAL_PREF are evaluated before AS_PATH length
Once an earlier, higher-priority attribute breaks the tie, later attributes including AS-path are never compared
Prepend lengthens AS-path only, so it is powerless against a higher weight or local-pref already set locally or by inbound policy
Prepend affects how the receiving AS chooses to reach you (inbound), not how your own router selects egress when local attributes dominate

confirmation_commands.purpose: Read the per-path attributes and the stated best-path reason to see which step decided it
Compare weight, local-pref, and AS-path across paths to find the deciding attribute
Check for a weight or local-preference being set that overrides the AS-path prepend

traps: Adding more prepends when weight or local-preference, not AS-path, is deciding best-path
Forgetting that prepend influences inbound choices at the neighbor, not local egress when local attributes dominate
Not reading the best-path reason, which states exactly which attribute won
Overlooking an inbound route-map that sets local-pref and silently outranks the prepend
